name: gcp-run-execution-poll
description:
POLL a Cloud Run Job Execution via 'GET https://run.googleapis.com/v2/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/locations/{REGION}/jobs/{JOB_NAME}/executions/{EXECUTION_NAME}' and derive a coarse status: 'succeeded' (succeededCount>=1, failedCount==0, completionTime present), 'failed' (failedCount>=1), or 'running' (otherwise). Companion to 'GCP: Run Cloud Run Job' — feed it the execution_id that the execute step returns. EXECUTION_NAME accepts either the short id or a full 'projects/.../executions/X' resource path (the last segment is extracted).

This module does NOT block; it returns the current snapshot with a `done` boolean. Drop it inside a workflow wait-until-done loop (add_wait_node + a loop / confidence-gate that re-polls until done==true), so the workflow owns the cadence. Once done, use 'GCP: Fetch Job Logs' to read the run output.

Auth is the Phase D impersonated-token path: the controller mints a short-lived (~10 min) impersonated service-account access token at dispatch and injects it under 'gcp/impersonated/<sa_email>/access_token' (granted via '/api/gcp/connect-full'). Set AUTH_HEADER to that vault:// reference wrapped in 'Bearer '.

AUTH_HEADER.description: vault:// path to the Phase D impersonated SA access token wrapped in 'Bearer ' (e.g. 'Bearer vault://gcp/impersonated/talos-runner@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com/access_token'). Set <sa_email> to your talos-runner service account. The controller mints the impersonated token at dispatch. Least privilege: set the module's allowed_secrets to the EXACT path, not the 'gcp/impersonated/*' wildcard.

PROJECT_ID.description: GCP project id that owns the Cloud Run Job. Must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{4,28}[a-z0-9]$ (validated before the request is built).

REGION.description: Cloud Run region (location) of the job, e.g. us-central1 or europe-west1. Must match ^[a-z]+-[a-z]+[0-9]$ (validated before the request is built).

JOB_NAME.description: Short name of the Cloud Run Job whose execution is being polled. Must match ^[a-z]([-a-z0-9]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$ (validated before the request is built).

EXECUTION_NAME.description: The execution to poll — the 'execution_id' returned by 'GCP: Run Cloud Run Job'. Accepts either the short id or a full 'projects/.../executions/X' path (the last segment is used). Must match ^[a-z]([-a-z0-9]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$ after extraction (validated before the request is built).

setup_instructions.title: Run the job first
setup_instructions.detail: Execute the Cloud Run Job with 'GCP: Run Cloud Run Job'. Capture its returned 'execution_id' (or full 'execution') and pass it here as EXECUTION_NAME.

setup_instructions.title: Use inside a wait-until-done loop
setup_instructions.detail: This module returns a snapshot with a `done` boolean; it does NOT block. Wrap it in a workflow loop (add_wait_node for the delay + a loop / confidence-gate re-polling until done==true). Terminal statuses are 'succeeded' and 'failed'.

setup_instructions.title: Auth (same as the execute step)
setup_instructions.detail: Set AUTH_HEADER to 'Bearer vault://gcp/impersonated/<sa_email>/access_token' (full-tier consent via '/api/gcp/connect-full'). The runner SA needs read access to the job (roles/run.viewer or roles/run.developer). On 401s, re-consent or refresh the underlying full-tier token.

setup_instructions.title: Fetch logs once done
setup_instructions.detail: When done==true, run 'GCP: Fetch Job Logs' with the same EXECUTION_NAME to read the execution's stdout/stderr from Cloud Logging.
